{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7347","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7347/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7347/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/7347/events","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/7347","id":2626428467,"node_id":"I_kwDOCJ0aI86cjB4z","number":7347,"title":"Layer.reset_view Issue on Scaled Images","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1022379511,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDIyMzc5NTEx","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/labels/bug","name":"bug","color":"d73a4a","default":true,"description":"Something isn't working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-10-31T09:48:18Z","updated_at":"2024-11-01T06:20:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### 🐛 Bug Report

Is it possible that there is race condition related to layer.reset_view or something like that. I am noting that it sometimes fails to reset to view to the scaled version of the image (it resets the view as if the scale is 1). So far, I am unable to reproduce it consistently and I am going a bit crazy so I thought that I write here.

I have been trying different versions of python and napari. I am not able to reproduce the error at all in 0.4.9.

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

- Make sure that the scale is not 1, e.g., using `viewer.layers[0].scale = [10,10]` in the console
- Press reset the view button in gui or using `viewer.reset_view()`

Note that this sometimes works to reproduce and sometimes it doesn't. The wrong behavior is that the view is resetted as if the scale is [1,1]

p.s. i am testing with RGB images but i wouludn't think that matters.

### 💡 Expected Behavior

_No response_

### 🌎 Environment

napari: 0.5.4, 0.5.0
Python: 3.10 and 3.11
OS: Windows 10


### 💡 Additional Context

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38/events","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38","id":2623721822,"node_id":"I_kwDOEGU9S86cYtFe","number":38,"title":"RGB OME-TIFF and Blending","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-10-30T11:06:21Z","updated_at":"2024-11-18T12:40:30Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I am generating simple RGB OME-TIFFs. Everything is working as expected with saving the file using tifffile and napari-tiff is recognizing them as RGB images (`PHOTOMETRIC.RGB`) with one channel and `SamplesPErPixel` of 3.

The issue is that napari-tiff defaults to adding blending metadata as "additive" even for a simple RGB image. This means opening two of them in napari will mess things up. This is not the expected behaviour. The expected blending in this case is "translucent_no_depth" or "translucent" (i don't know the difference 🤣). 

As far as I can see, this is not related to the ome-tiff file metadata and it is just related to how napari-tiff decides what blending to use. That being said, I can be mistaken.

The relevent line is:
https://github.com/napari/napari-tiff/blob/8c704181275bc21e2ba220e4bd1bf44a97b204c4/napari_tiff/napari_tiff_metadata.py#L340

Attaching an example image here
[wolf.ome.tif.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17571686/wolf.ome.tif.zip)
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/comments/2482931939","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38#issuecomment-2482931939","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38","id":2482931939,"node_id":"IC_kwDOEGU9S86T_ojj","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-18T12:40:29Z","updated_at":"2024-11-18T12:40:29Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@jni just got arround playing with this:

First, regarding RGB images, I think it is important to distingush between SamplesPerPixels (which is 3 in the case of RGB) and Channel (which is 1 in the case of RGB). The other important aspect is the `PHOTOMETRIC.RGB` flag in OME-TIFF metadata.

Based on these definitions, an RGB channel is one where we have 3/4 samples per pixel and the photometric.rgb flag is set (without relying on magic, e.g., assuming it is RGB if we have 3 channels even if photometric.rgb flag is wrong).

That being said, Let's discuss some possibilities:

**1. One channel grayscale image**
I don't have a preference but in my mind, the default should be translucent.

**2. Multiple channels gray scale image**
I think this is the case where additive is expected and multiple layers should be used?

**3. One channel RGB image**
I expect that the image will be loaded as 1 translucent layer.

**4. Multiple channels and each channel is RGB (I deal with images of this nature):**
In my use case, my preference is that the image is loaded as translucent and as 1 layer only (because the images are colocated and it is easy to switch between them using the slider or anlyze them as a group). That being said I see the argument that it should be multiple layers and each one is translucent. Do you have use cases where it should be additive here?

**5. Multiple mix of channels and each channel is either grayscale or RGB.**
I don't have use case for this. My mind tells me translucent with each channel (be it RGB or grayscale) as a seperate layer.

Ho do you want to handle each case? I can give it a go once it is decided.

An `IndexError: list index out of range` is triggered when data is cleared in the shapes layer (using code) while a shape is selected. I believe this is because the selection code is attempting to find the shape somehow.

The current workaround is to manually set the selection to empty before clearing.

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

1. Add shapes layer and a shape in it.
2. Make sure one of the shapes is selected
3. Attempts to clear the data in the layer using `layer.data = []`

### 💡 Expected Behavior

Data is cleared normally (without error) without having manually (using code) to clear the selection.

### 🌎 Environment

- Windows 10 and 11 (64 bit)
- napari 5.4 (and also tested on the latest main branch)

### 💡 Additional Context

References and relevant issues
Followup on https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/6849#issuecomment-2095676630

# Description
The idea is to have our own custom handler for log messages so we can then expose the logs as we prefer from the GUI. This will make it easier for users to copy-paste logs even if they didn't manually open from the console, and also easier for developers to quickly check things.

I never really worked with the logging module, so I have a feeling that this is garbage... but oh well, let's see how bad :P

To test, open napari and do some stuff. Then, from the terminal or console run:
```py
napari._LOG_STREAM.logs_at_level(10)
```

EDIT: Added a very quick-and-dirty GUI for the logger based on the implementation of the `About` widget. For how bad it is, it's actually ok xD You can open it from the menu `Help -> Show log".

cc @czaki

Everything is working as expected with saving the file using tifffile and napari-tiff is recognizing them as RGB images (`PHOTOMETRIC.RGB`).

The issue is that napari-tiff defaults to adding blending metadata as "additive" even for a simple RGB image. This means opening two of them in napari will mess things up. This is not the expected behaviour. The expected blending in this case is "translucent_no_depth". As far as I can see, this is not related to the ome-tiff file metadata and it is just related to how napari-tiff decides what blending to use.

The relevent line is:
https://github.com/napari/napari-tiff/blob/8c704181275bc21e2ba220e4bd1bf44a97b204c4/napari_tiff/napari_tiff_metadata.py#L340","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/38/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

Is it possible that there is race condition related to layer.reset_view or something like that. I am noting that it sometimes fails to reset to view to the scaled version of the image (it resets the view as if the scale is 1). So far, I am unable to reproducing it consistently and I am going a bit crazy so I thought that I write here.

I have been trying different versions of python and napari. I am not able to reproduce the error at all in 0.4.9.

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

- Make sure that the scale is not 1, e.g., using `viewer.layers[0].scale = [10,10]` in the console
- Press reset the view button in gui or using `viewer.reset_view()`

Note that this sometimes works to reproduce and sometimes it doesn't. The wrong behavior is that the view is resetted as if the scale is [1,1]

p.s. i am testing with RGB images but i wouludn't think that matters.

### 💡 Expected Behavior

_No response_

### 🌎 Environment

napari: 0.5.4, 0.5.0
Python: 3.10 and 3.11
OS: Windows 10


### 💡 Additional Context

Tifffile is not detecting that by default either. This causes napari-tiff to have `is_rgb` as false because of this line
`is_rgb = series.keyframe.photometric == PHOTOMETRIC.RGB and nchannels in (3, 4)`

Once I fixed my export pipeline, the images are displayed as RGB as expected.

You can close this issue for now. I suspect that more complex images will still require SamplesPerPixels variable to subdivide them correctly but not for simple cases.

p.s. thinking about subdividing the channels into groups, the only way I can think of is to have all the C have the same number of SamplesPerPixels in order for it to be reshaped correctly. I don't know how to do it otherwise.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari-tiff/issues/comments/2352362660/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

It is also probably related to the tiff reader upstream (tifffile) but I am not sure. 

When reading a tiff file with jpeg compression in napari, napari creates a temperary file "turbojpegxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.dll" in the temp directory of the user (c:\users\username\appdata\local\temp\). A new file with different numbering in the filename is created everytime you restart napari.

## To Reproduce
Load TIFF file into napari using the builtin reader.

## Expected behavior
If it is a needed file, why not have it packaged with napari. A dll is usually not a temperary file. Also, why create multiple copies every time.

## Environment
napari: 0.4.18
platform: windows 10
Python: 3.10.2
Qt: 5.15.2

 - Any other relevant information:

## Additional context

A public facing API to block thumbnail update. This API would work in any layer.

## Motivation
I am trying to squeeze every bit of performance for live image preview in napari. Currently, I am utilizing `layer.data = new_image` function which also updates the thumbnail. 

## Pitch
`layer.is_thumbnail_update` as a boolean that can be set by widgets/plugins or something like that. If it is set to false, than the thumbnail is not updated when data is changed.

Note that there is already some mechanism that is similar in the Shapes layer only.

## Alternatives
`layer._update_thumbnail = lambda : ...` would disable the thumbnail update for the time being but this is private API.

## Additional context
There might be a need for a special API to update the data with minimal triggering of optional features (thumbnail update).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/6657/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/6657/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/comments/1980132127","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/6657#issuecomment-1980132127","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/6657","id":1980132127,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCJ0aI852Bmsf","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-06T05:49:14Z","updated_at":"2024-03-06T05:49:14Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I am not sure how the thumbnail is currently generated but if it is a resize of the image then that is a large overhead for large 2D images for little gain. if it that is the case, maybe it is better to generate it **lazely** as a snapshot from the viewer.

Initial gamma values are not respected in the viewer. It is shown in the slider correctly but it is not applied in the viewer. Interestingly, it is applied in the thumbnail preview.

### 💡 Steps to Reproduce

Run the following in the napari console. You will not that the gamma slider has changed but it is not applied on the image.
```python
from skimage import data
viewer.add_image(data.cell(), name='cell', gamma=.5)
````


### 💡 Expected Behavior

Gamma values are respected in the viewer

### 🌎 Environment

napari: latest in pypi (4.19)
OS: Windows 10 and 11, 64bit
Python: 3.10 (64bit)

### 💡 Additional Context

Access to the vispy node from a public API, specifically the `attach` function in the Vispy ImageVisual. I believe this is related to the public qt_viewer being deprecated. 

## Motivation
I am designing camera acqusition plugin. As part of the design I am implementing a very simple white balance filter (as a start) but I expect other filters and algorithms to be added for live analysis.

## Alternatives
There is no alternatives that are usable. I've testing implementing this on the CPU during the frame acqusition and it is slows down the FPS considerably. I've implemented it as a filter in Vispy and it works perfectly.

## Additional context
GPU related functionlity for plugin developer should be part of the design of napari. This includes running filters, getting data from GPU. A public facing API to block thumbnail update. This API would work in any layer.

## Motivation
I am trying to squeeze every bit of performance for live image preview in napari. Currently, I am utilizing `layer.data = new_image` function which also updates the thumbnail. 

## Pitch
`layer.is_thumbnail_update` as a boolean that can be set by widgets/plugins or something like that. If it is set to false, than the thumbnail is not updated when data is changed.

Note that there is already some mechanism that is similar in the Shapes layer only.

## Alternatives
`layer._update_thumbnail = lambda : ...` would disable the thumbnail update for the time being but this is private API.

## Additional context
There might be a need for a special API to update the data with minimal triggering of optional features (thumbnail update).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/6657/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/6657/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

request","color":"65187A","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2023-04-27T11:37:46Z","updated_at":"2023-11-02T05:48:20Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Description:

It would be great if regionprops have the ability to estimate the: 1) maximum inscribed circle, and 2) minimum circumscribed circle. The output for each would be a diameter/radius and a center.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10503349/234850667-97a22d79-800c-45a7-a6f6-caf5205c1a04.png)


# Alternatives
The properties can be estimated by extracting the mask for each identified label (e.g., using the boundaries of the label obtained using region props) and passing it to the functions below. The code is modified from https://gist.github.com/DIYer22/f82dc329b27c2766b21bec4a563703cc

```python
def maximum_inscribed_circle(mask: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, np.ndarray]:
    """Gets the maximum inscribed circle"""
    dist_map = cv2.distanceTransform(mask.astype(np.uint8), cv2.DIST_L2, cv2.DIST_MASK_PRECISE)
    _, radius, _, center = cv2.minMaxLoc(dist_map)
    diameter = 2 * radius
    return diameter, center

def minimum_circumscribed_circle(mask: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, np.ndarray]:
    """returns the diameter of the minimum circumscribed circle"""
    contours, _ = cv2.findContours(mask.astype(np.uint8), cv2.RETR_TREE, cv2.CHAIN_APPROX_SIMPLE)[-2:]
    center, radius = cv2.minEnclosingCircle(np.concatenate(contours, 0))
    diameter = 2 * radius
    return diameter, center

```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/6912/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/6912/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/comments/1790108332","html_url":"https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/6912#issuecomment-1790108332","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/6912","id":1790108332,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB6-0c5qsuKs","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2023-11-02T05:48:20Z","updated_at":"2023-11-02T05:48:20Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"@soupault 

The common definitions for circularity and roundness are orthogonal. Circularity/sphercity(in 3D) defines how much the shape resembles a circle (e.g., an elipse is elongate is not circular). Roundness studies the actual angles of the grains. So you can have that is low and the other high or vice versa.

![Rounding_ _sphericity_EN](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/assets/10503349/240111f4-c9ca-4016-9d1c-27a09ad129cf)

Now coming to the quantitative definitions for each: there are 10s of definitions over the last century or so. So, from point of view, it is better to focus scikit-image measurments on the primitive measurments (e.g., perimeter, area, maximum inscribed circle, and minimum circumscribed circle) rather than force one definition of these high-level metrics.  Nobody can dispute the primetive measurments.

Implementing a specific metric for roundness or circularity is straightfoward if you decided on an equation for them. That should be the job of the user, not scikit-image.

It is also probably related to the tiff reader upstream (tifffile) but I am not sure. 

When reading a tiff file with jpeg compression in napari, napari creates a temperary file "turbojpegxxxxxxxxxxxxxx.dll" in the temp directory of the user (c:\users\username\appdata\local\temp\). A new file with different numbering in the filename is created everytime you restart napari.

## To Reproduce
Load TIFF file into napari using the builtin reader.

## Expected behavior
If it is a needed file, why not have it packaged with napari. A dll is usually not a temperary file. Also, why create multiple copies every time.

## Environment
napari: 0.4.18
platform: windows 10
Python: 3.10.2
Qt: 5.15.2

 - Any other relevant information:

## Additional context

Description:

It would be great if regionprops have the ability to estimate the: 1) maximum inscribed circle, and 2) minimum circumscribed circle. The output for each would be a diameter/radius and a center.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10503349/234850667-97a22d79-800c-45a7-a6f6-caf5205c1a04.png)


# Alternatives
The properties can be estimated by extracting the mask for each identified label (e.g., using the boundaries of the label obtained using region props) and passing it to the functions below.

```python
def maximum_inscribed_circle(mask: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, np.ndarray]:
    """Gets the maximum inscribed circle"""
    dist_map = cv2.distanceTransform(mask.astype(np.uint8), cv2.DIST_L2, cv2.DIST_MASK_PRECISE)
    _, radius, _, center = cv2.minMaxLoc(dist_map)
    diameter = 2 * radius
    return diameter, center


def minimum_circumscribed_circle(mask: np.ndarray) -> tuple[float, np.ndarray]:
    """returns the diameter of the minimum circumscribed circle"""
    contours, _ = cv2.findContours(mask.astype(np.uint8), cv2.RETR_TREE, cv2.CHAIN_APPROX_SIMPLE)[-2:]
    center, radius = cv2.minEnclosingCircle(np.concatenate(contours, 0))
    diameter = 2 * radius
    return diameter, center

```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/6912/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scikit-image/scikit-image/issues/6912/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

request"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2022-04-02T14:35:46Z","updated_at":"2022-08-21T09:40:59Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## 🚀 Feature
It would be cool to have dock widgets which don't have a close-button.

## Motivation

I'm having issues with workflows in napari where the user closed dock widgets after configuring them. If the user would only hide the dock widget, everything would be cool.

## Pitch

The Dockwidget class used in napari has a constructor parameter `close_btn` which would allow to have dock widgets without a close button ([see](https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/6a3e11aa717e7928a0a5a3c7693577729a466ef1/napari/_qt/widgets/qt_viewer_dock_widget.py#L77))

However, this parameters is not available when calling `viewer.window.add_dock_widget(...)`. It would be practical if the parameter was also available in the API (e.g. [here](https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/427ac948ef9dcb1890ae3d69024a51c756f16747/napari/_qt/qt_main_window.py#L720-L729)).

## Alternatives

At the moment, I'm doing it like this ([see](https://github.com/haesleinhuepf/napari-assistant/blob/d1664848a843d546efa1c953a0d215579f6f9077/napari_assistant/_gui/_Assistant.py#L176)):

```
dw = viewer.window.add_dock_widget(...)
dw._close_btn = False
```

And it works fine. It would be cool though if this was possible without accessing private class members.

## Additional context

Btw. I tried to hide the close button by changing the `features` of the DockWidget as described in the QT documentation. This didn't work, as napapri is using a custom header with custom buttons. It might make sense to mention in the documentation (e.g. of [setFeatures](https://github.com/napari/napari/blob/6a3e11aa717e7928a0a5a3c7693577729a466ef1/napari/_qt/widgets/qt_viewer_dock_widget.py#L215)) that the functionality described here doesn't work in napari: https://doc.qt.io/qt-5/qdockwidget.html#DockWidgetFeature-enum","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4348/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4348/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/comments/1221509659","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/4348#issuecomment-1221509659","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4348","id":1221509659,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCJ0aI85IzsIb","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-08-21T09:40:59Z","updated_at":"2022-08-21T09:40:59Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Here is another case where closing messes up things:

1. Add dock_widget using `dock_widget = viewer.window.add_dock_widget(module_widget, name="My Widget", menu=viewer.window.window_menu)`
2. Run napari. Widget is visible and its menu action is there in the `window` menu.
3. Close widget.
4. Menu action is gone 😔

This is expected as close removes the widget completely from memory. It is just not something that I want from a design perspective.

solution as stated in the forum.image.sc: use `dock_widget._close_btn=False`

implements the QCheckComboBox that is discussed in #89.  A few notes:

- `addItems` and `addItem` included `checked` parameters to quickly initializes the items while adding. We can remove that if you feel that it doesn't follow the "zen" of qt.
- Quite a few convenience functions are added to the widget to retrieve and control the items, e.g., get the indices of all checked items, set checked states of all items.
- The label of the combobox has two modes: 1) static set by the user or a list of all selected items. The example included showcases this feature.
- Still getting to know qtbot for actual interface testing. I was planning on using it for `_handleItemPressed` test but I failed. For now, it is being tested like any other function.

As always, I am open to suggestions and modifications. In terms of functionality, this is what I needed for my use case but others might have other ideas.

p.s. I see that the list of comboboxes in superqt is growing (enum, search) which is great.

This is a low priority thing anyway.

# Use of Check Combobox
Check Combobox is pretty standard in control libraries nowadays. It goes by many names though (multiple select comobobox, check combobox, ...). Here are some examples for different programming languages:
- In Javascript (web application), here is an example for the framework `Vue`. The Vuetify combobox allows for multiple selection through the use of check comobox (https://vuetifyjs.com/en/components/combobox/#multiple-combobox)
- In C# world, again multiple combobox (https://www.syncfusion.com/winforms-ui-controls/combobox/multiselect-combobox). This example is using winforms framework which has been around since early 2000s. Other newer frameworks (WPF, UWP, and WinUI) have equivalents.

Some notes:
- Some implementations allows the user to type to filter the list (similar to autocomplete behavior).
- Some implementations allows the user to remove items directly from the main list/label of the combobox (each item has an x button) (https://vuetifyjs.com/en/components/combobox/#advanced-custom-options)

# Double Click Issue
I agree that is a weird behavior that I didn't catch It is actually a remanent of the original comobox. It shouldn't behave that way. I suggest I remove the behavior and make it do what is expected: double clicking on the item should check/uncheck twice. That is the behavior that is usually implemented in other ones (https://vuetifyjs.com/en/components/combobox/#multiple-combobox)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/issues/comments/1173324212/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

for Easy Multiple Items Selection","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"This implements the QCheckComboBox that is discussed in #89.  A few notes:

- `addItems` and `addItem` included `checked` parameters to quickly initializes the items while adding. We can remove that if you feel that it doesn't follow the "zen" of qt.
- Quite a few convenience functions are added to the widget to retrieve and control the items, e.g., get the indices of all checked items, set checked states of all items.
- The label of the combobox has two modes: 1) static set by the user or a list of all selected items. The example included showcases this feature.
- Still getting to know qtbot for actual interface testing. I was planning on using it for `_handleItemPressed` test but I failed. For now, it is being tested like any other function.

As always, I am open to suggestions and modifications. In terms of functionality, this is what I needed for my use case but others might have other ideas.

p.s. I see that the list of comboboxes in superqt is growing (enum, search) which is great.

implements the QCheckComboBox that is discussed in #89.  A few notes:

- `addItems` and `addItem` included `checked` parameters to quickly initializes the items while adding. We can remove that if you feel that it doesn't follow the "zen" of qt.
- Quite a few convenience functions are added to the widget to retrieve and control the items, e.g., get the indices of all checked items, set checked states of all items.
- The label of the combobox has two modes: 1) static set by the user or a list of all selected items. The example included showcases this feature.
- Still getting to know qtbot for actual interface testing. I was planning on using it for `_handleItemPressed` test but I failed. For now, it is being tested like any other function.

As always, I am open to suggestions and modifications. In terms of functionality, this is what I needed for my use case but others might have other ideas.

p.s. I see that the list of comboboxes in superqt is growing (enum, search) which is great.

implements the QCheckComboBox that is discussed in #89.  A few notes:

- `addItems` and `addItem` included `checked` parameters to quickly initializes the items while adding. We can remove that if you feel that it doesn't follow the "zen" of qt.
- Quite a few convenience functions are added to the widget to retrieve and control the items, e.g., get the indices of all checked items, set checked states of all items.
- The label of the combobox has two modes: 1) static set by the user or a list of all selected items. The example included showcases this feature.
- Still getting to know qtbot for actual interface testing. I was planning on using it for `_handleItemPressed` test but I failed. For now, it is being tested like any other function.

As always, I am open to suggestions and modifications. In terms of functionality, this is what I needed for my use case but others might have other ideas.

p.s. I see that the list of comboboxes in superqt is growing (enum, search) which is great.

Yes, I agree that is a big bug that I need to fix. My guess is that this was added when I introduced the fix to the double click. I felt it was a hacky way of doing it and I was not sure why it worked 🤣

I will work on this week and get back to you. I want to check on icon, signal and so on in other comments too.

For documentation, honestly, I didn't know that the md files existed. implements the QCheckComboBox that is discussed in #89.  A few notes:

- `addItems` and `addItem` included `checked` parameters to quickly initializes the items while adding. We can remove that if you feel that it doesn't follow the "zen" of qt.
- Quite a few convenience functions are added to the widget to retrieve and control the items, e.g., get the indices of all checked items, set checked states of all items.
- The label of the combobox has two modes: 1) static set by the user or a list of all selected items. The example included showcases this feature.
- Still getting to know qtbot for actual interface testing. I was planning on using it for `_handleItemPressed` test but I failed. For now, it is being tested like any other function.

As always, I am open to suggestions and modifications. In terms of functionality, this is what I needed for my use case but others might have other ideas.

p.s. I see that the list of comboboxes in superqt is growing (enum, search) which is great.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/pulls/comments/930626181","pull_request_review_id":1051896473,"id":930626181,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOFkBxis43eDqF","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,153 @@
+from enum import Enum, auto
+from typing import Any, List, Union
+
+from qtpy.QtCore import QEvent, Qt
+from qtpy.QtGui import QStandardItem
+from qtpy.QtWidgets import QComboBox, QStyle, QStyleOptionComboBox, QStylePainter
+
+
+class QCheckComboBox(QComboBox):
+    """
+    A combobox with a check for each item inserted.
+    based on https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47575880/qcombobox-set-title-text-regardless-of-items
+    """
+
+    class QCheckComboBoxLabelType(Enum):
+        """Label type"""
+
+        STRING = auto()
+        SELECTED_ITEMS = auto()
+
+    _label_text: str = "Select items"
+    _label_type: QCheckComboBoxLabelType = QCheckComboBoxLabelType.STRING
+
+    def __init__(self) -> None:
+        """Initializes the widget"""
+        super().__init__()
+        self.view().pressed.connect(self._handleItemPressed)
+        self.view().doubleClicked.connect(self._handleItemPressed)
+        self._changed = False
+
+    def _update_label_text_with_selected_items(self) -> None:
+        checked_indices = self.checkedIndices()
+        selected_text_list = []
+        for index in checked_indices:
+            selected_text_list.append(self.itemText(index))
+        self.setLabelText(", ".join(selected_text_list))
+
+    def setLabelText(self, label_text: str) -> None:
+        """Sets the label text"""
+        self._label_text = label_text
+        self.repaint()
+
+    def labelText(self) -> str:
+        return self._label_text
+
+    def setLabelType(self, label_type: QCheckComboBoxLabelType) -> None:
+        """Sets the label type"""
+        self._label_type = label_type
+        if label_type == QCheckComboBox.QCheckComboBoxLabelType.SELECTED_ITEMS:
+            self._update_label_text_with_selected_items()
+
+    def labelType(self) -> QCheckComboBoxLabelType:
+        """Returns label type"""
+        return self._label_type
+
+    def _handleItemPressed(self, index: int) -> None:
+        """Updates item checked status"""
+        item = self.model().itemFromIndex(index)
+        if item.checkState() == Qt.Checked:
+            item.setCheckState(Qt.Unchecked)

implements the QCheckComboBox that is discussed in #89.  A few notes:

- `addItems` and `addItem` included `checked` parameters to quickly initializes the items while adding. We can remove that if you feel that it doesn't follow the "zen" of qt.
- Quite a few convenience functions are added to the widget to retrieve and control the items, e.g., get the indices of all checked items, set checked states of all items.
- The label of the combobox has two modes: 1) static set by the user or a list of all selected items. The example included showcases this feature.
- Still getting to know qtbot for actual interface testing. I was planning on using it for `_handleItemPressed` test but I failed. For now, it is being tested like any other function.

As always, I am open to suggestions and modifications. In terms of functionality, this is what I needed for my use case but others might have other ideas.

p.s. I see that the list of comboboxes in superqt is growing (enum, search) which is great.

Selection","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-06-09T22:00:12Z","updated_at":"2022-06-13T15:47:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/pulls/91","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/superqt/pull/91","diff_url":"https://github.com/napari/superqt/pull/91.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/napari/superqt/pull/91.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This implements the QCheckComboBox that is discussed in #89.  A few notes:

- `addItems` and `addItem` included `checked` parameters to quickly initializes the items while adding. We can remove that if you feel that it doesn't follow the "zen" of qt.
- Quite a few convenience functions are added to the widget to retrieve and control the items, e.g., get the indices of all checked items, set checked states of all items.
- The label of the combobox has two modes: 1) static set by the user or a list of all selected items. The example included showcases this feature.
- Still getting to know qtbot for actual interface testing. I was planning on using it for `_handleItemPressed` test but I failed. For now, it is being tested like any other function.

As always, I am open to suggestions and modifications. In terms of functionality, this is what I needed for my use case but others might have other ideas.

p.s. I see that the list of comboboxes in superqt is growing (enum, search) which is great.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/issues/94","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/issues/94/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/issues/94/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/issues/94/events","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/superqt/issues/94","id":1277056170,"node_id":"I_kwDOFkBxis5MHlSq","number":94,"title":"QLabeledRangeSlider no labels after setValue() int the second time","user":{"login":"looninho","id":9430758,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk0MzA3NTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9430758?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho","html_url":"https://github.com/looninho","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/looninho/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2022-06-20T14:58:02Z","updated_at":"2022-06-20T16:32:53Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**update values the second time removes all labels**
When I create my_slider QLabeledRangeSlider object then set the values with my_slider.setValue(mylist_1) every thing works fine but when I set a new dataset with my_slider.setValue(mylist_2), only handles updated all labels disappear.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. create a QWidget to host the QLabeledRangeSlider slider
2. create a QLabeledRangeSlider object as child then set its value with .setValue() method
3. create a QPushButton to set new values then connect its signal clicked to an update method
4. see error: the slider updates with no label at each handle, only range label displayed.

**example code**

```
import sys, os

from PySide6.QtWidgets import QApplication, QWidget, QPushButton
from PySide6.QtCore import Qt
from superqt import QLabeledRangeSlider

class Window(QWidget):
  def __init__(self, parent=None):
    super(Window, self).__init__(parent)
    sl = tuple([0, 90, 210, 259, 374])

    self.main_slider = QLabeledRangeSlider(parent=self)
    self.main_slider.setOrientation(Qt.Horizontal)
    width = self.geometry().width()
    self.main_slider.setMinimumWidth(width)

    self.main_slider.setRange(sl[0], sl[-1])
    self.main_slider.setValue(tuple(sl))

    btn = QPushButton(self)

    btn.clicked.connect(self.update)


  def update(self):
    sl = tuple([1261, 1281, 1334, 1365, 1697, 1871, 1916])
    self.main_slider.setRange(sl[0], sl[-1])
    self.main_slider.setValue(sl)


if __name__ == '__main__':
  app = QApplication([])
  win = Window()
  win.show()
  sys.exit(app.exec())
```

**Expected behavior**
expected labels .at each handle.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9430758/174621850-5a8c00f9-0bfc-4688-8159-e987fd508b95.png)


**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Windows 10

This is useful in some scenarios (e.g., select multiple samples).  If there is interest, I will clean up my current code, add tests and create an initial pull request for it.

I think it deserves a place in superqt as the default combobox: 
- has a relatively awkward (non-pythonic) way of adding checkable item. It needs multiple lines and you cannot use the `additem` function. 
- Does not change the checked states of the items when clicked
- Automatically when you select any item.
- Has the last selected item as a title.

The above can be fixed by overriding some of the combobox in qt. A good starting point is [https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47575880/qcombobox-set-title-text-regardless-of-items](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/47575880/qcombobox-set-title-text-regardless-of-items). There is still things to add to it to make it more user friendly (e.g., `additem` override).

**Screenshots**
The top one is the default combobox. The bottom one is one possible implementation of CheckComboBox.

![python3 9_j59ge6Raij](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10503349/168439532-25abc1a4-5e32-48f2-a394-ee0778d4e7cd.gif)

1. I guess "Default" is confusing. Yes, I mean the standard Qt Combobox implementation with checkbox items added inside it instead of qlabel or string. Basically, adding a bunch of standard code and expecting it to work.

2. The use case for this: If you have a tens of items (e.g., sample 1, sample 2, sample 3) and you want the user to select the ones to run the analysis or something. This means that most of the time, the combobox would be filled dynamically based on the data. For selecting options for algorithms and such, I agree that that a group of checkboxes is the way to go. 

I will clean up the code, write tests, and make a draft PR. I just wanted to make sure that it is of interest as every line of code is possible bug waiting to happen in a library, e.g., I see that you fixed quite a few items in QCollapsible :) ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/superqt/issues/comments/1131195815/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/12782","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/12782/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/12782/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/issues/12782/events","html_url":"https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/12782","id":687459511,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU2ODc0NTk1MTE=","number":12782,"title":"Add `compositional` to `scipy.stats` for compositional data analysis","user":{"login":"jolespin","id":9061708,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjkwNjE3MDg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9061708?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin","html_url":"https://github.com/jolespin","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jolespin/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":37340722,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzNzM0MDcyMg==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/labels/scipy.stats","name":"scipy.stats","color":"0366d6","default":false,"description":""},{"id":37340735,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzNzM0MDczNQ==","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/scipy/scipy/labels/enhancement","name":"enhancement","color":"bfe5bf","default":true,"description":null}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2020-08-27T18:43:53Z","updated_at":"2022-05-29T11:12:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Absolutely.  Compositional data analysis [CoDA] is large in fields such as bioinformatics, geology, and economics.  

> In statistics, compositional data are quantitative descriptions of the parts of some whole, conveying relative information. Mathematically, compositional data is represented by points on a simplex. Measurements involving probabilities, proportions, percentages, and ppm can all be thought of as compositional data.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Compositional_data

**Describe the solution you'd like**
To have a `compositional` section in `scipy.stats` that, at the very least, has common CoDA methods such as closure, center log-ratio, isometric log-ratio, etc.  Currently some of the methods are implemented in [`scikit-bio`](http://scikit-bio.org/docs/0.5.6/generated/skbio.stats.composition.html#module-skbio.stats.composition) but I feel that they are much more generalizable to more sciences.

There are also correlation-style pairwise operations that are robust to bias from compositionality.  [This figure sums up why this is important](https://msystems.asm.org/content/msys/2/1/e00162-16/F1.large.jpg?width=800&height=600&carousel=1) from Morton et al. 

One of the most practical pairwise operations is the `rho` metric originally published in [Lovell et al. 2015](https://journals.plos.org/ploscompbiol/article?id=10.1371/journal.pcbi.1004075), adapted by [Erb et al. 2016](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4870310/), and implemented in R by [Quinn et al. 2018](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-017-16520-0) in the [propr](https://github.com/tpq/propr) R package.  I've reimplemented key metrics such as `rho`, `phi`, and `variance log-ratio` in my [`compositional`](https://github.com/jolespin/compositional) Python package that have been optimized to make use of vectorization in `numpy`.  `rho` is a drop-in replacement for correlation where the values range from -1 to 1 and `phi` is the unscaled version of `rho`.  `variance log-ratio` is akin to a distance measure I believe. 

I would like for these to be integrated into the `scipy` ecosystem to be more accessible to not only bioinformaticians but geologist and other sciences that use compositional data. Currently, most of the implementations either use many dependencies, do not fully make use of numpy vectorization for speed, or are available only in R.  

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

I've been using 3rd party packages (`scikit-bio` and `gneiss`) and developed my own (https://github.com/jolespin/compositional).

**Additional context (e.g. screenshots)**

This figure is also helpful in describing the rationale: 
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9061708/91481871-f77b2e80-e859-11ea-8b5f-44a8040fe339.png)

> Fig 1. Why correlations between relative abundances tell us absolutely nothing.
> These plots show two hypothetical mRNAs that are part of a larger total. (a) Seven pairs of relative abundances (mRNA1/total, mRNA2/total) are shown in red, representing the two mRNAs in seven different experimental conditions. The dotted reference line shows (mRNA1 + mRNA2)/total = 1.) Rays from origin through the red points show absolute abundances that could have given rise to these relative abundances, e.g., the blue, green or purple sets of points (whose Pearson correlations are −1, +1 and 0.0 respectively). (b) Relative abundances that are proportional must come from equivalent absolute abundances. Here the blue, green or purple sets of point pairs have the same proportionality as the pairs of relative abundances in red, though not necessarily the same order or dispersion. 

https://journals.plos.org/ploscompbiol/article/figure/image?size=large&id=10.1371/journal.pcbi.1004075.g001


Key resources: 
* [Gloor et al. 2017](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fmicb.2017.02224/full)
* [Quinn et al. 2018](https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article/34/16/2870/4956011)
* [Lovell et al. 2020](https://academic.oup.com/nargab/article/2/2/lqaa040/5859926)
* [Quinn et al. 2019](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6755255/)
* [Lovell et al. 2015](https://journals.plos.org/ploscompbiol/article?id=10.1371/journal.pcbi.1004075)

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4419","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4419/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4419/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4419/events","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/4419","id":1215697176,"node_id":"I_kwDOCJ0aI85IdhEY","number":4419,"title":"Feature proposal: *spaces*","user":{"login":"brisvag","id":23482191,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIzNDgyMTkx","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/23482191?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag","html_url":"https://github.com/brisvag","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/brisvag/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1022379513,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMDIyMzc5NTEz","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/labels/feature","name":"feature","color":"a2eeef","default":false,"description":"New feature or request"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-04-26T09:50:08Z","updated_at":"2022-04-28T03:38:20Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## 🚀 Feature

### The problem
- Often datasets are composed of many different images that do not share a coordinate space (i.e: no reason/way to spatially or temporally relate them along a dimension); here I'm calling these separate world "*spaces*".
- One often wants to have access to all the *spaces* without creating/deleting layers every time (and reading from disk every time)
- It's often useful to quickly switch between *spaces* to compare features between samples.

### Current approaches
1. simply load all the layers in the layerlist. This has 3 major downsides:
    - you have to toggle layer visibility to switch between *spaces*, which is a nightmare (especially if multiple layers share the same space, and linking them is out of question). This can be simplified with a widget (I currently [do that in blik](https://github.com/gutsche-lab/blik/blob/e175659d212b5a96d6868668aa780286ce7a068c/blik/widgets/volume_selector.py#L51-L71)) but it's not ideal.
    - the layerlist is basically useless once many layers are loaded, cause it's impossible to find what you need and gives no useful visual cuues to the state of the viewer. (*this last part will be partially solved by [layer groups](https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/4018), but not the general problem of having many __spaces__*)
    - If you have many layers (> hundreds), opening the whole dataset can take **minutes**. Time taken is [no longer exponential](https://github.com/napari/napari/pull/1945), but still unusable in case of large datasets. This probably won't be addressed by the async efforts, because the issue is not with data loading but with vispy node creation and hooking up and firing of events (afaik).
 2. create a widget that creates/inserts layers into the layer list on demand. This used to be my previous solution in `blik`.
    - does not have the problems above, because only the layers that are actually viewed are added to the viewer, making it easier to see what's what and faster to load
    - however, it's not a napari native solution, which means that
        - we can't use reader plugins to create the layers because they woudl all be added at once
        - the user/plugin is forced to keep track of everything that does not stay in the viewer (which means holding a reference to some sort of "dataset" object
        - bye bye serialization

### The Solution
Make some form of point `2.` a first class citizen in napari.
- The viewer should be aware of *spaces* (or workspaces/scenes/worlds/whatever) which are collections of layers that are dimensionally unrelated to one-another but live in the same viewer
- Make it easy to see what *spaces* exist and to switch between them

Potentially, once layer groups are a thing, *spaces* could simply be the nodes at depth 1 in the layer graph.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4419/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4419/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/comments/1111708003","html_url":"https://github.com/napari/napari/issues/4419#issuecomment-1111708003","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/4419","id":1111708003,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCJ0aI85CQ1Fj","user":{"login":"MosGeo","id":10503349,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTAzMzQ5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10503349?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo","html_url":"https://github.com/MosGeo","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/MosGeo/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-04-28T03:38:20Z","updated_at":"2022-04-28T03:38:20Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"This would be good for for datasets with different modality and scales, e.g., if you have 3D CT scan and SEM of the same sample.

I also see this as a precursor to a 'project' system.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/napari/napari/issues/comments/1111708003/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Description
<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.

Description
<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.

<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.

<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.

<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.

Description
<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.

+"""
+A generic lock class to be used as a locking mechanism.
+"""
+from enum import Enum
+from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
+
+from pydantic import BaseModel, validator
+
+from napari.utils.translations import trans
+
+
+class LockMode(Enum):
+    """Lock modes"""
+
+    EXACT = 0
+    IN_LIST = 1
+    IN_RANGE = 2
+    LARGER_THAN = 3
+    SMALLER_THAN = 4
+
+
+class ValueNotCompatibleWithLockMode(Exception):
+    """Custom error that is raised when the value is not compatible with lock mode"""
+
+    def __init__(self, value, message: str) -> None:
+        self.value = value
+        self.message = f"value: {value}. " + message
+        super().__init__(message)
+
+
+class AttributeNotFound(Exception):
+    """Custom error that is raised when the attribute is not found in the locker"""
+
+    def __init__(self, message: str) -> None:
+        self.message = message
+        super().__init__(message)
+
+
+class Lock(BaseModel):
+    """
+    A lock consists of components:
+    1. A boolean to house the status of the lock (open, locked).
+    2. A value of the lock, for example, an attribute maximum can be locked to certain number.
+    3. A lock mode to desginate the type of lock (soft (user), or hard (code and user))
+    4. comments
+    """
+
+    value_lock_mode: LockMode = LockMode.EXACT
+    value: Any = None
+    locked: bool = True
+    hard_lock: bool = True
+    comments: Optional[str] = None
+
+    @validator("value_lock_mode")
+    def value_lock_mode_lock_mode_valid(cls, value):
+        """Validate value lock mode"""
+        return value

+    def value_lock_mode_valid(cls, value, values, **kwargs):
+        """Validate value"""
+        value_lock_mode = values["value_lock_mode"]
+

+            """Quick checking if it is numeric type"""

<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.

<!-- What does this pull request (PR) do? Why is it necessary? -->
<!-- Tell us about your new feature, improvement, or fix! -->
<!-- If your change includes user interface changes, please add an image, or an animation "An image is worth a thousand words!" -->
<!-- You can use https://www.cockos.com/licecap/ or similar to create animations -->
This is the pull request for the implementation discussed in #3466. It is a locking mechanism that can be used anywhere in napari.

## Architecture
Two classes: Lock and Locker. Locker is composed of a number of locks (dictionary). The locks are pydantic model with validation on the parameters. The lock itself can accomdate multiple locking mode (exact, in list, in range, larger than, and smaller than).  There is also a hard lock (which is currently implemented in the setattribute in the layer automatically. And soft lock (if needed) as discussed in #3466.

## What's missing (enahancement)
- I tried adding the same setattribute to Evented model but it failed the tests. I don't know why as I am not familiar with EventedModel and its use.
- As disccussed in #3466, I am still not sure how to implement ownership and automatic release of locks once the owner is closed. I am thinking we can tab in to the plugin life cycle for this but I am not familiar with that.
- Currently, all functions are public but some can be safely made private i think depending on the above enhancement.

Any help is appreciated with the two enhancements.
